symptoms.symptom_name: Interdigital scaling, white soggy maceration and painful fissuring between the toes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Interdigital maceration and scaling, worst in the lateral web spaces, is the hallmark of interdigital dermatophytosis.
Contact dermatitis favours the dorsum and contact surfaces rather than the moist interdigital clefts.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus and burning, worse on removing footwear, with strong foot odour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritus and malodour from macerated, dermatophyte-colonised web spaces are typical of symptomatic tinea pedis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thickened, dry, scaly (hyperkeratotic, moccasin-type) soles and lateral borders

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse plantar and lateral-border hyperkeratotic scaling is the moccasin pattern of chronic dermatophyte infection, often coexisting with the interdigital pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serous oozing from deep interdigital fissures (portal for bacterial entry)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fissuring with serous ooze reflects severe maceration; clear serous fluid (not pus) is consistent with uncomplicated tinea rather than bacterial superinfection.
Fissures are the entry point; serous (not purulent) ooze without other signs keeps bacterial infection low probability but on the safety-net list.
Communal wet floors are a classic source of dermatophyte transmission and raise pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no systemic upset; not diabetic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/systemic symptoms and of diabetes lowers the probability and consequence of secondary bacterial infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spreading erythema, no ascending lymphangitic streaks, foot not diffusely hot; pedal pulses strong and equal, sensation intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of spreading demarcated erythema, warmth and ascending streaks on clinician examination excludes cellulitis at this encounter; intact pulses/sensation exclude a critically ischaemic or neuropathic foot. Clinician-measured — sealed; the AI Doctor approximates this by asking the patient about spreading redness/warmth/streaks.

symptoms.symptom_name: KOH microscopy showing branching dermatophyte hyphae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Branching septate hyphae on KOH confirm dermatophyte infection, but the test is not required when the clinical picture is characteristic and would not be available in a telehealth chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scraping for KOH microscopy and fungal culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for a clinically characteristic tinea pedis; reserve for diagnostic doubt or treatment failure. The AI Doctor should not delay simple topical treatment to obtain it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Random blood glucose / HbA1c if recurrent or poorly healing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for undiagnosed diabetes in recurrent or poorly-healing foot infections; not urgent in this encounter but reasonable at routine GP follow-up.
